prompt: I need to migrate the KotlinConf App KMP project (github.com/JetBrains/kotlinconf-app) to AGP 9.0. Currently on AGP 8.11.2. There are three KMP library modules (shared, ui-components, core) each using kotlin.multiplatform + com.android.library. The androidApp module uses kotlin.multiplatform + com.android.application. Please analyze and migrate the entire project.
source: JetBrains/kotlinconf-app — 3 KMP library modules (Path A) + androidApp with kotlinMultiplatform (Path B), uses Compose Multiplatform, Google Services, aboutlibraries
agent_rubric: All three KMP library modules (shared, ui-components, core) have com.android.library replaced with com.android.kotlin.multiplatform.library
The android {} blocks in shared, ui-components, and core are nested inside kotlin { } (not at top level)
The androidApp module no longer applies kotlin.multiplatform — it uses only com.android.application (AGP 9 has built-in Kotlin support)
The androidApp module's sources are moved from androidMain to main (or the source set structure is updated for single-platform)
The version catalog has a plugin alias for com.android.kotlin.multiplatform.library
AGP version in gradle/libs.versions.toml is updated to 9.0.0 or higher
Compose Multiplatform integration still works with the new plugin configuration
The migration does not break the dependency structure between modules (androidApp depends on shared, shared depends on core and ui-components)
